commit.message:
fix: redirect embedded static asset paths to root

Cached pages with old relative asset references cause browsers to
resolve _content/ and _framework/ paths against the page URL, producing
URLs like /movie/.../discs/_content/Foo/bar.js. These match greedy
Razor route templates and cause 404s with wasted DB queries.

The LowercaseUrlMiddleware now detects /_content/ and /_framework/
appearing at non-root positions and 301-redirects to the correct
root-relative path (e.g. /_content/Foo/bar.js).

commit.message:
Fix TTL entry merges to use max() instead of last-writer-wins

Add merge_ttl_current() helper to LedgerDelta that takes
max(live_until_ledger_seq) for TTL entries during Updated+Updated
coalescing, while preserving last-writer-wins for all other entry types.

This fixes two consensus-critical bugs:
- Cross-cluster: delta.merge() Updated+Updated for TTL keys now takes max
- Intra-cluster: record_update() Updated→Updated for TTL keys now takes max

Matches stellar-core's maybeMergeRoTTLBumps semantics. TTL key with
non-TTL data is treated as a hard error (invariant violation).

Includes 10 regression tests covering both orderings, non-TTL unchanged
behavior, Created+Updated not max-merged, and pipeline-level scenarios
for intra-cluster, cross-cluster, and RW-after-RO cases.

Closes #1512

fix: skip auth middleware for OAuth discovery paths (#186)

* fix: skip auth middleware for /.well-known/ OAuth discovery paths

MCP clients (including Copilot CLI) probe /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server
to detect OAuth. When the auth middleware returned 401 on this path, clients
incorrectly concluded OAuth was required and prompted for Client ID.

By skipping auth on /.well-known/ paths, these probes receive 404 from the
SSE app, signaling that simple Bearer token auth should be used instead.

commit.message:
feat(generator): uppercase aggregate and window function names (#31)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR was authored by GitHub Copilot CLI (powered by Claude Sonnet
4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Implements uppercase aggregate and window function names as specified in
#28.

**Before:**
```sql
SELECT
   count(*)
  ,sum(amount)
  ,row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY id ORDER BY ts)
  ,array_agg(DISTINCT sku ORDER BY sku)
FROM t
```

**After:**
```sql
SELECT
   COUNT(*)
  ,SUM(amount)
  ,ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY id ORDER BY ts)
  ,ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT sku ORDER BY sku)
FROM t
```

Scalar functions remain lowercase: `regexp_extract`, `strftime`,
`read_parquet`, etc.

## Implementation

- `src/jarify/generator.py`: Added `_UPPERCASE_FUNCS:
ClassVar[frozenset[str]]` derived from sqlglot's `AggFunc` subclass
hierarchy plus `RowNumber`. A supplemental set covers DuckDB dialect
renames (`var_pop`, `quantile_cont`, `listagg`, `bool_or`, etc.).
`normalize_func` override checks this set and uppercases/lowercases
accordingly. `arrayagg_sql` hardcoded `"array_agg"` in two places —
replaced with `self.normalize_func("ARRAY_AGG")`.
- `tests/fixtures/duckdb/agg_window_uppercase.{input,expected}.sql`: New
fixture pair covering the full range of aggregate and window functions.
- Updated 4 existing fixture snapshots.
- `docs/sql-style-guide.md`: Renamed "Functions — lowercase" section to
"aggregate and window uppercase, scalar lowercase" with updated
examples. Fixed `count`, `row_number`, `array_agg` references
throughout.

Resolves #28

Three instruction files for GitHub Copilot and AI assistants:

1. .github/copilot-instructions.md — General overview
   - What DynamicData is and why it matters
   - Why performance and Rx compliance are critical
   - Repository structure (public API surface, not internals)
   - Operator architecture pattern (extension method -> internal class -> Run())
   - Thread safety principles
   - Breaking change policy
   - Testing patterns

2. .github/instructions/rx-contracts.instructions.md — Comprehensive Rx guide
   - Core concepts: composability, hot vs cold, Publish/RefCount
   - The Observable contract (serialized notifications, terminal semantics)
   - Scheduler guide: all common schedulers, injection for testability
   - Complete Disposable helper guide: Disposable.Create, CompositeDisposable,
     SerialDisposable, SingleAssignmentDisposable, RefCountDisposable,
     BooleanDisposable, CancellationDisposable
   - Writing custom operators (single-source and multi-source patterns)
   - Operator review checklist
   - Common pitfalls (cold re-subscribe, leak, sync-over-async, Subject exposure)

3. .github/instructions/dynamicdata-operators.instructions.md — Operator guide
   - Changeset model and change reasons
   - Complete operator catalog with detailed code examples:
     Filtering, Transformation, Sorting, Paging, Grouping, Joining,
     Combining, Aggregation, Fan-out/Fan-in, Refresh, Lifecycle,
     Buffering, Binding, Utilities

The list-vs-cache guidance belongs in the main copilot-instructions.md
where it serves as a top-level navigation aid, not buried in the list
operator file. Also consolidates the instruction file links into the
new section.

* Split testing into main + cache + list instruction files

Testing section was too cache-specific. Now split into three:

- copilot-instructions.md (Testing section, 73 lines): Philosophy,
  requirements, frameworks, Rx contract validation, regression test
  rules, stress test principles, utilities reference, domain types,
  anti-patterns — all universal, no cache/list specifics

- testing-cache.instructions.md (175 lines, NEW): Both observation
  patterns (AsAggregator + RecordCacheItems), cache changeset
  assertions, TestSourceCache, stub/fixture pattern, cache stress
  tests, per-operator test checklist

- testing-list.instructions.md (143 lines, NEW): List aggregator,
  RecordListItems, list changeset assertions (item vs range changes),
  cache-vs-list testing differences table, list fixture pattern,
  list stress tests, per-operator test checklist

* Add ObservableChangeSet.Create docs, instruction maintenance rule

Cache instructions: Added ObservableChangeSet.Create section with
8 overloads documented, 3 practical examples (sync event bridge,
async API loading, SignalR live updates), key behaviors explained.

List instructions: Added ObservableChangeSet.Create<T> section with
sync and async examples (FileSystemWatcher, async stream).

Main instructions: Added 'Maintaining These Instructions' section
requiring new operators to be added to instruction files, operator
behavior changes to update tables, new utilities/domain types to
be documented.

* Add deterministic completion pattern and stress test guidelines

Testing instructions updated with three new subsections:

- Waiting for Pipeline Completion: Publish + LastOrDefaultAsync + ToTask
  pattern. Never use Task.Delay to wait for pipelines to settle.

- Chaining Intermediate Caches: AsObservableCache() to materialize stages,
  Connect() to chain, completion cascades through the chain.

- Stress Test Pattern: Full 7-step pattern for stress testing operators:
  deterministic seed data, chained pipelines, multi-threaded writers,
  pre-computed LINQ expectations, exact content verification.

Anti-patterns updated: Task.Delay for settling → completion pattern.

* Potential fix for pull request finding

* Make UTF-8

* docs: add composition-first Rx guidance with Defer pattern examples

Captures the principle that Observable.Create with manual observer
forwarding is a code smell — prefer declarative composition using
Defer, Do, Merge, Concat, and other existing operators. Includes
concrete before/after examples and criteria for when Observable.Create
IS appropriate.

* docs: strengthen Rx contract trust — axioms, not guidelines

The contracts are unconditional guarantees. Doubting them and adding
safety wrappers abandons the very thing that makes Rx code correct
by construction.

commit.message:
Forms: Fix form block not working when Blocks module is inactive (#48037)

* Forms: Fix form block not working when Blocks module is inactive

The Form block was unusable when the contact-form module was active but
the blocks module was not. Three barriers prevented it from working:

1. The parent jetpack/contact-form block was only registered via the
   Blocks module's extension loader. Now registered directly from the
   Forms package in Contact_Form_Plugin::register_contact_form_blocks().

2. The editor JS depended on jetpack-blocks-editor, a script only
   registered by the Blocks module. Without it, WordPress silently
   skipped loading the Forms editor bundle entirely.

3. The JS availability check (Jetpack_Editor_Initial_State) and module
   status check (useModuleStatus) both defaulted to "unavailable" when
   the Blocks module didn't provide the initial state, showing an
   "Activate Forms" placeholder instead of the actual block.

The fix registers a minimal jetpack-blocks-editor script with the
required initial state (available_blocks, modules, feature_flags) when
the Blocks module is inactive, allowing Forms to work independently.

commit.message:
Fix gh attestation verify using mutually exclusive flags (#82)

Remove --signer-repo flag from gh attestation verify invocations in both
ProvenanceVerifier.cs and install-copilotd.sh. The gh CLI treats
--signer-repo and --signer-workflow as mutually exclusive flags in the
same flag group (cert-identity, cert-identity-regex, signer-repo,
signer-workflow). Using both causes attestation verification to always
fail with: 'if any flags in the group [...] are set none of the others
can be'.

Keep --signer-workflow as it is more specific (constrains both the repo
and the exact workflow file) and provides stronger provenance guarantees.

commit.message:
fix(decomposition): implement all-or-nothing child creation with rollback

When run_decomposition() creates child items, partial failures now trigger
a rollback of all successfully created children, preventing orphaned items
with incomplete scope.

Previously, if 2 of 3 subtasks succeeded, the parent would be marked
'auto-decomposed' and removed from the ready queue, leaving the missing
subtask's work silently lost. The created children covered only partial
scope but the parent was marked as fully decomposed.

Changes:
- Added _delete_child_item() helper to clean up items
- Modified run_decomposition() to track failed subtasks
- On any failure, rollback all created children before returning
- Updated parent metadata/stats only on full success
- Added tests for delete function and rollback behavior
- Updated existing test to expect correct all-or-nothing behavior

Fixes: PokePoke-s8t0k

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(workflow): protect cleanup_on_failure in finally block from masking exceptions

Wrap cleanup_on_failure() in try/except in the finally block of
process_work_item(). Previously, if cleanup_on_failure() raised (e.g.,
git timeout, file lock), it masked the original exception and skipped
unregister_agent(). Now cleanup failures are caught and logged, ensuring:
- The original exception propagates unchanged
- unregister_agent() always executes
- Cleanup errors are still visible in logs

Also condense a few lines to stay within file length limits.
Update tests to expect caught (not propagated) cleanup exceptions.
